title: Merge Bases, Topology, and Integration Strategies · History Defense
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for merge bases, topology, and integration strategies and requires the smallest safe merge topology and strategy decision record with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for merge topology and strategy decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compute one or multiple best common ancestors, octopus bases, fork points, and independent tips for complex graphs.
Choose fast-forward, no-ff, ff-only, or refusal from release policy and graph evidence rather than branch-name folklore.
Evaluate ort strategy behavior and bounded options for renames, whitespace, subtree shifts, and favoring conflict hunks without replacing semantic review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Gita Merge Base Multiple · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Case G12 is a quiz in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy.

Which action demonstrates Gita Merge Base Multiple without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compute one or multiple best common ancestors, octopus bases, fork points, and independent tips for complex graphs.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:

stimulus.title: Gita Merge Result Defense failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A conflict-free merge proves both sides remain behaviorally compatible.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Gita Merge Result Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G19 is a quiz in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication.
